label: Compulsions
prompts: Are there any actions or routines I feel driven to repeat to reduce discomfort?
What sensations or emotions show up right before I feel the urge to act?
What thought or image tends to trigger a compulsive action?
How does the urge to act show up in my body—tightness, restlessness, pressure?
What story do I tell myself about what will happen if I don’t act on a compulsion?
What feels “incomplete” or unresolved in the moment before I take an action?
How intense is my drive to act, and how does it change over time?
What do I notice about my attention—does it narrow, speed up, or fixate throughout the day?
What internal rules or expectations seem to be driving compulsions?
What do I feel I have to do (rituals, checking, repeating thoughts) to feel okay?
What am I afraid of happening if I don't engage with a compulsion?
What emotions feel hardest to sit with when the urge appears?
What smaller signals tell me the compulsion is starting to build?
How do I talk to myself during the urge—reassuring, demanding, fearful, neutral?
What assumptions am I making about the consequences of not acting?
What parts of the compulsion feel automatic, and what parts feel deliberate?
What do I notice about the difference between the urge and the action?
How do I feel immediately after completing an action I felt I had to do—physically, mentally, emotionally?
How long does any sense of relief or change last?
What patterns do I notice in how my drive to act feels—time of day, stress level, environment?
What alternative explanations could exist for why an urge appeared at that moment?
What influences from my past might shape how I interpret my needs?
What influences from my current environment might be contributing to my urges or actions?
What would I like my relationship with my own action or inaction to look like over time?
What values or goals feel connected to the situation that triggers compulsive urges?
What parts of my life feel most uncertain or ambiguous?
What do I notice about the pace of my thinking throughout the day?
How do I relate to myself in the moments after something I feel driven to do passes?
